gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/354)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/354)
text: كَتَبَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ إِلَى أَبِي مُحَمَّدٍ ( عليه السلام ) رَجُلٌ حَلَفَ بِالْبَرَاءَةِ مِنَ اللَّهِ وَ مِنْ رَسُولِهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَحَنِثَ مَا تَوْبَتُهُ وَ كَفَّارَتُهُ فَوَقَّعَ ( عليه السلام ) يُطْعِمُ عَشَرَةَ مَسَاكِينَ لِكُلِّ مِسْكِينٍ مُدٌّ وَ يَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ .

en.hubeali: ‘Muhammad Bin Al-Hassan wrote to Abu Muhammad<sup>asws</sup>, ‘A man swore with the disavowing from Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, and from His<sup>azwj</sup> Rasool<sup>saww</sup>, then he reneged. So what is his repentance and his expiation?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> wrote: ‘He should feed ten poor ones, for each poor, a handful, and he should seek Forgiveness of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic’.
en.sarwar: 7. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated the following: “Muhammad ibn al-Hassan once wrote to abu Muhammad, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, that a man has sworn to disown Allah and the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, and he then opposed his oath and about what is his repentance and expiation. He (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, signed the answer that said, ‘He must feed ten destitute people with one mud (a certain measurement) to each one and ask forgiveness from Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious.’”
